identity.short_name: Matrix-polynomial invariants with defined eigenvalue symbols and mod-1000 output

subdomain(s): traces and determinants as eigenvalue invariants

problem_spec.summary: Given a fixed 4x4 integer matrix A, define matrices B,C,D through explicit matrix polynomials, with an integer parameter Phi defined by a floor involving det(B), tr(C), and det(A). Define mu_i, sigma_i, rho_i as the eigenvalues of B,C,D (with algebraic multiplicity), and compute an aggregate S built from sums of these eigenvalues (and squares), then output S mod 1000.
givens: D = A^3 - Phi A^2 + Phi^2 A - Phi^3 I.
mu_1..mu_4 are the eigenvalues of B (algebraic multiplicity).
sigma_1..sigma_4 are the eigenvalues of C (algebraic multiplicity).
rho_1..rho_4 are the eigenvalues of D (algebraic multiplicity).
ask: Compute S = (sum_{i=1}^4 mu_i^2) + (sum_{i=1}^4 sigma_i) + (sum_{i=1}^4 rho_i) + det(A) (mod 1000), reported as a three-digit string.

parameter(s).name: dimension
parameter(s).purpose: fixes trace(I)=4 and the number of eigenvalues counted

parameter(s).name: poly_C
parameter(s).purpose: defines C and links its trace to tr(B^2) and tr(B)

parameter(s).name: floor_rule_for_Phi
parameter(s).purpose: defines an integer parameter from invariants

invariant(s).name: eigenvalue_symbol_definition
invariant(s).statement: The symbols mu_i, sigma_i, rho_i denote the eigenvalues of B, C, D respectively, each counted with algebraic multiplicity (so sums over i equal traces).

invariant(s).name: spectral_mapping_for_matrix_polynomials
invariant(s).statement: If lambda_i are eigenvalues of A, then mu_i=p(lambda_i) are eigenvalues of B=p(A), and rho_i=lambda_i^3-Phi lambda_i^2+Phi^2 lambda_i-Phi^3 are eigenvalues of D.

invariant(s).name: sum_rho_equals_trace_D
invariant(s).statement: sum_{i=1}^4 rho_i = tr(D) = tr(A^3)-Phi tr(A^2)+Phi^2 tr(A)-4 Phi^3.

invariant(s).name: factorization_shortcut_for_detB
invariant(s).statement: Since p(t)=(t-1)(t-2), one has B=p(A)=(A-I)(A-2I), hence det(B)=det(A-I)det(A-2I).

invariant(s).name: floor_parameter_well_defined
invariant(s).statement: The denominator tr(C)+det(A) is nonzero, so Phi is well-defined as an integer.

essential_assumption(s): Eigenvalue symbols are explicitly defined as eigenvalues with algebraic multiplicity.
Standard identities: tr(M)=sum eigenvalues, tr(M^2)=sum (eigenvalues)^2, det(M)=product eigenvalues.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Given A, all derived invariants tr(A^k), det(A), det(A-I), det(A-2I) are uniquely determined. These uniquely determine tr(B), tr(B^2), tr(C), Phi, and tr(D), hence S and S mod 1000 are unique.

technique(s).name: trace_eigenvalue_translation
technique(s).purpose: replace eigenvalue sums by traces tr(B^2), tr(C), tr(D)

technique(s).name: trace_expansion_for_B_and_B_squared
technique(s).purpose: expand B and B^2 as polynomials in A and compute traces via tr(A^k)

technique(s).name: determinant_via_polynomial_factorization
technique(s).purpose: compute det(B) using B=(A-I)(A-2I) and determinant multiplicativity

technique(s).name: floor_parameter_evaluation
technique(s).purpose: compute Phi exactly as a floor of a rational number

technique(s).name: compute_trace_of_D
technique(s).purpose: compute tr(D) by linearity of trace

technique(s).name: modular_postprocessing
technique(s).purpose: reduce S mod 1000 and format as three digits

theorem(s).name: trace_equals_sum_of_eigenvalues
theorem(s).role: converts sums of eigenvalues to traces

theorem(s).name: eigenvalues_of_polynomial_in_matrix
theorem(s).role: supports spectral mapping and the factorization shortcut

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: similarity invariants (trace/det) allow spectral sums without diagonalization

critical_trick(s): Define eigenvalue symbols explicitly, then replace eigenvalue sums by traces.
Use the factorization (t-1)(t-2) to compute det(B) via det(A-I)det(A-2I).
Compute Phi by an exact floor, then compute tr(D) and finish with a mod-1000 reduction.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: exact integer matrix arithmetic for traces/determinants and verification

task(s).name: exact_matrix_power_and_trace
task(s).reason: compute tr(A^k) for k=1..4 and confirm tr(B), tr(B^2), tr(C), tr(D)

task(s).name: exact_determinants
task(s).reason: compute det(A), det(A-I), det(A-2I), det(B) exactly

task(s).name: end_to_end_mod_check
task(s).reason: compute S and S mod 1000; confirm formatted answer "076"
